title: Information-normalized repair candidate
notes: Twenty-third-round mutation generated after edit-distance acceptability showed that raw repair-neighbour distance is too coarse. It tests whether repair distance must be normalized by target sentence length or information mass before predicting acceptability. It is an unscored successor candidate, not a scoped module.

edges.rationale: Higher raw distance raises normalized repair distance when target information mass is held fixed.
Higher target information mass lowers the normalized cost of the same raw repair distance.
Higher normalized repair distance makes the intended sentence or message harder to recover.
Higher normalized repair distance tends to lower felt naturalness in noisy-channel acceptability tasks.
Recovering the intended form or message can increase felt naturalness.
Felt naturalness can surface as reported acceptability in rating tasks.
Task format affects whether respondents rate literal form status, reconstructed meaning, or ordinary acceptability.
Literal-analysis, proofreading, and first-pass acceptability frames change grammar attribution.
Community licensing remains attribution evidence, but normalized repair distance is not licensing.
Reported acceptability is contextual evidence for attribution and can be inflated by noisy-channel reconstruction.
